creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) Microsoft, Google, Amazon say Anthropic Claude remains available to non-defense customers
dc:creator: Julie Bort, Rebecca Bellan
content: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.
contentSnippet: Trump's Department of War feud with Anthropic won't impact other companies that are using Claude via Microsoft and Google products.

creator: Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude found 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox over two weeks
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."
contentSnippet: In a recent security partnership with Mozilla, Anthropic found 22 separate vulnerabilities in Firefox — 14 of them classified as "high-severity."

title: (No AI) The AI Doc is an overwrought hype piece for doomers and accelerationists alike
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![A bearded man wearing glasses making a face of discomfort at someone off-camera.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-06-at-1.32.13%E2%80%AFPM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Co-director Daniel Roher. | Image: Focus Features

We are in the thick of [a massive push](https://www.theverge.com/tech/863365/national-retail-federation-show-shopping-commerce-ai) to [incorporate generative AI](https://www.theverge.com/2024/1/13/24035152/ces-generative-ai-hype-robots) into almost [every aspect of our lives](https://www.theverge.com/23610427/chatbots-chatgpt-new-bing-google-bard-conversational-ai), but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole that comes across like fantastical ad copy. And the rate at which AI firms release new iterations of their products can make it hard to keep track of what's going on in the industry as a whole.

In [_The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist_](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/880120/the-ai-doc-or-how-i-became-an-apocaloptimist-trailer), codirectors Daniel Roher and Charlie Tyrell attempt to make sense of this moment in gen AI's rise to prominence The f …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/890806/the-ai-doc-or-how-i-became-an-apocaloptimist-review)
contentSnippet:
Co-director Daniel Roher. | Image: Focus Features	

We are in the thick of a massive push to incorporate generative AI into almost every aspect of our lives, but it is still easy to be confused about what it is and how it works. It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole that comes across like fantastical ad copy. And the rate at which AI firms release new iterations of their products can make it hard to keep track of what's going on in the industry as a whole.
In The AI Doc: Or How I Became an Apocaloptimist, codirectors Daniel Roher and Charlie Tyrell attempt to make sense of this moment in gen AI's rise to prominence The f …
Read the full story at The Verge.

It doesn't help that many of gen AI's proponents and detractors both speak about it with a feverish hyperbole […]

title: (No AI) The Pentagon formally labels Anthropic a supply-chain risk
author: Tina Nguyen
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/gettyimages-2263863571.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a press conference on US military action in Iran, at the Pentagon in Washington, DC, on March 2, 2026. | Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images

After weeks of [failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/886082/ai-vs-the-pentagon-killer-robots-mass-surveillance-and-red-lines), the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court.

The decision, [first reported by _The Wall Street Journal_ on Thursday](https://www.wsj.com/politics/national-security/pentagon-formally-labels-anthropic-supply-chain-risk-escalating-conflict-ebdf0523?mod=rss_Technology), citing one source familiar, will bar defense contractors from working with the government if they use Claude, Anthropic's AI program, in their products. Though the designation is typically applied to foreign companies with ties to adversarial governments, this is the first time that an American company …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/890347/pentagon-anthropic-supply-chain-risk)

US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a press conference on US military action in Iran, at the Pentagon in Washington, DC, on March 2, 2026. | Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images	

After weeks of failed negotiations, public ultimatums, and lawsuit threats, the Defense Department has formally labeled Anthropic a "supply-chain risk," escalating its fight with the AI company over their acceptable use policies and potentially bringing their fight to court.
The decision, first reported by The Wall Street Journal on Thursday, citing one source familiar, will bar defense contractors from working with the government if they use Claude, Anthropic's AI program, in their products. Though the designation is typically applied to foreign companies with ties to adversarial governments, this is the first time that an American company …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The decision, first reported by The Wall Street Journal on Thursday, citing one source familiar, will […]

title: (No AI) Codex Security: now in research preview
content: Codex Security is an AI application security agent that analyzes project context to detect, validate, and patch complex vulnerabilities with higher confidence and less noise.
contentSnippet: Codex Security is an AI application security agent that analyzes project context to detect, validate, and patch complex vulnerabilities with higher confidence and less noise.

title: (No AI) How Descript enables multilingual video dubbing at scale
content: Descript uses OpenAI models to scale multilingual video dubbing, optimizing translations for both meaning and timing so dubbed speech sounds natural across languages.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.
contentSnippet: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.

creator: Steven Levy
title: (No AI) Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
dc:creator: Steven Levy
content: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”
contentSnippet: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”
